questions.question: What is manufacturing?
questions.correct_answer: The production of goods in large quantities by processing raw materials into finished products
questions.explanation: Manufacturing converts raw materials like minerals or agricultural products into useful goods.

questions.question: Why are manufacturing industries important for a country?
questions.correct_answer: They promote economic growth, provide employment, and increase the value of raw materials
questions.explanation: Manufacturing industries strengthen the economy and support other sectors like agriculture.

questions.question: What are agro-based industries?
questions.correct_answer: Industries that use agricultural products as raw materials
questions.explanation: Examples include cotton textile, sugar, and jute industries.

questions.question: What are mineral-based industries?
questions.correct_answer: Industries that use minerals and metals as raw materials
questions.explanation: Examples include iron and steel, aluminium, and cement industries.

questions.question: Why is the iron and steel industry called a basic industry?
questions.correct_answer: Because it provides raw materials for many other industries
questions.explanation: Steel is used in construction, transport, machinery, and many other sectors.

questions.question: What are the main raw materials required for steel production?
questions.correct_answer: Iron ore, coking coal, and limestone
questions.explanation: These materials are essential for producing steel in blast furnaces.

questions.question: What is the raw material used for aluminium production?
questions.explanation: Bauxite is processed and refined to produce aluminium metal.

questions.question: Name one major center of the automobile industry in India.
questions.explanation: Other centres include Delhi, Pune, Bengaluru, and Mumbai.

questions.question: What types of pollution are caused by industries?
questions.correct_answer: Air pollution, water pollution, land pollution, and noise pollution
questions.explanation: Industrial waste and emissions contaminate the environment.

questions.question: What is one method to reduce industrial pollution?
questions.correct_answer: Treat industrial waste before releasing it into the environment
questions.explanation: Effluent treatment and recycling help reduce environmental damage.

notes.title: Manufacturing
notes.content: Manufacturing is the process of converting raw materials into finished goods on a large scale.

notes.title: Classification of Industries
notes.content: Industries can be classified based on raw materials, ownership, capital investment, and weight of products.

notes.title: Cotton Textile Industry
notes.content: The cotton textile industry is important for employment and export earnings in India.

notes.title: Iron and Steel Industry
notes.content: Iron and steel industries supply raw materials to many other industries.

notes.title: Industrial Pollution
notes.content: Industries cause pollution through emissions, chemical waste, and noise, affecting the environment and health.

facts.fact: Manufacturing industries are considered the backbone of economic development.
India is one of the largest producers of cotton textiles in the world.
Steel production is often used as an indicator of a country’s economic development.
Aluminium is lightweight, corrosion resistant, and a good conductor of electricity.
